entities.name: HETERORHABDITIS SPP.
entities.description: Entomopathogenic nematodes isolated from north-western Himalaya, India, tested for pathogenicity against diamondback moth larvae.

entities.name: PLUTELLA XYLOSTELLA
entities.description: The diamondback moth, a lepidopteran pest whose larvae were tested for susceptibility to various entomopathogenic nematodes.

entities.name: NORTH WESTERN HIMALAYA
entities.description: The geographical region in India where the indigenous entomopathogenic nematodes were isolated.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study's nematode isolation site is located.

entities.name: LABORATORY CONDITIONS
entities.description: The controlled environment used to study the efficacy of nematodes against P. xylostella larvae.

entities.name: H. INDICA
entities.description: A commercially available entomopathogenic nematode used as a comparative standard for efficacy.

entities.name: H. BACTERIOPHORA
entities.description: An indigenous isolate of entomopathogenic nematode (HRJ) that achieved 100% mortality in P. xylostella larvae.

entities.name: MORTALITY
entities.description: The death rate of P. xylostella larvae, which varied based on nematode dosage, exposure period, and larval stage.

entities.name: INTEGRATED MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: A management strategy for controlling P. xylostella that can incorporate the use of virulent EPN species.

entities.name: INFECTIVE JUVENILES
entities.description: The infectious life stage of entomopathogenic nematodes used for exposure and produced during reproduction within the host.
